# Let's open the provided file path and read raw content, show it, and find candidate spans.
path = "/mnt/data/file-CcY93dqrC8xwkSpU6rQR8L-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "rb") as f:
    data_bytes = f.read()
raw = data_bytes.decode('utf-8')
len(raw), raw[:4000]

ay1 = "أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ رَحِيمٌ"
raw.find(ay1)

start0 = raw.find(ay1)
end0 = start0 + len(ay1)
start0, end0, raw[start0:end0] == ay1, repr(raw[start0:end0])[:200]

ay1_indexes = [m.start() for m in __import__('re').finditer(ay1, raw)]
ay1_indexes

ay_variant = "أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتاً"
raw.find(ay_variant)

import re, unicodedata

raw = raw  # ensure variable

# find all double-quote segments
double_quote_positions = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', raw):
    content = m.group(1)
    # check if content has Arabic letters
    if re.search(r'[\u0600-\u06FF]', content):
        double_quote_positions.append((m.start(1), m.end(1), content))

# find all guillemets «...» segments
guil_positions = []
for m in re.finditer(r'«([^»]+)»', raw):
    content = m.group(1)
    if re.search(r'[\u0600-\u06FF]', content):
        guil_positions.append((m.start(1), m.end(1), content))

double_quote_positions[:20], guil_positions[:20], len(double_quote_positions), len(guil_positions)

for start,end,content in double_quote_positions:
    print("Span:", start, end, repr(content))
    print("Context:", repr(raw[start-40:end+40]))
    print()

# Prepare candidate spans: include ayah occurrences (ay1 at 124, 2407; ay_variant at 3361)
candidates = []

# Ayah occurrences: exact ay1 occurrences and variant
ay1 = "أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ رَحِيمٌ"
for idx in [m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(ay1), raw)]:
    candidates.append((idx, idx+len(ay1), ay1, 'unknown'))

ay_variant = "أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتاً"
v_idx = raw.find(ay_variant)
if v_idx != -1 and v_idx not in [c[0] for c in candidates]:
    candidates.append((v_idx, v_idx+len(ay_variant), ay_variant, 'unknown'))

# Add the quoted segments from double quotes and guillemets
for lst in (double_quote_positions, guil_positions):
    for (s,e,content) in lst:
        candidates.append((s,e,content,'unknown'))

# Sort candidates by start index
candidates = sorted(candidates, key=lambda x: x[0])

# Remove duplicates by exact identical start/end pair (some may coincide)
unique = []
seen = set()
for s,e,content,typ in candidates:
    if (s,e) not in seen:
        seen.add((s,e))
        unique.append((s,e,content,typ))
candidates = unique

# Print each candidate with context and check for cue words within ±64 chars
def find_cues(s,e):
    ctx = raw[max(0,s-64):min(len(raw),e+64)]
    ayah_cues = ['قال الله', 'قال الله تعالى', 'الله تعالى', 'سورة', 'الآية', 'قال تعالى', 'يقول الله', 'قال الله سبحانه', 'قال الله عز وجل', 'قال الله:' ,'قال الله تعالى:']
    hadith_cues = ['قال رسول', 'قال النبي', 'قال النبي ﷺ', 'روى', 'روي', 'روى عن', 'في الحديث', 'أخرجه', 'البخاري', 'مسلم', 'الترمذي','قال:','حيث قال','روى النبي','النبي ﷺ','قال صلى الله عليه وسلم']
    ay_found = [c for c in ayah_cues if c in ctx]
    had_found = [c for c in hadith_cues if c in ctx]
    return ctx, ay_found, had_found

for i,(s,e,content,_) in enumerate(candidates):
    ctx, ay_found, had_found = find_cues(s,e)
    print(f"{i}: start={s}, end={e}, len={e-s}, content_repr={repr(content)}")
    print("  trimmed raw snippet:", repr(raw[s:e]))
    print("  context +/-64:", repr(ctx))
    print("  ayah cues:", ay_found)
    print("  hadith cues:", had_found)
    print()

# Normalize trimming characters
trim_chars = set(list(" \n\r\t\u200f\u200e"  # whitespace incl directional marks
                     "«»\"'()[]{}<>،؛:.,!?…"  # punctuation and quotes
                     "ـ"  # tatweel
                    ))
# Also include special english punctuation '? ' if present - included above
# Implement trimming function
def trim_span(s,e):
    # Move s forward while raw[s] is in trim_chars
    # Move e backward while raw[e-1] is in trim_chars
    orig_s, orig_e = s, e
    while s < e and raw[s] in trim_chars:
        s += 1
    while e > s and raw[e-1] in trim_chars:
        e -= 1
    return s, e

final_spans = []
for (s,e,content,_) in candidates:
    # decide whether to include (we'll exclude 'وبعد' at 3907)
    if s == 3907:  # skip 'وبعد'
        continue
    # define classification by manual list per index start
    # We'll map start index to class as deduced earlier.
    # But let's determine class by cues: if ay_found present, classify Ayah else if had_found present, Hadeeth.
    ctx, ay_found, had_found = find_cues(s,e)
    if ay_found and not had_found:
        ctype = "Aya"
    elif had_found and not ay_found:
        ctype = "Hadeeth"
    elif ay_found and had_found:
        # If both present, classify by MATN itself: if MATN contains prayer to Allah 'اللّٰه' or 'يَا' or typical ayah markers maybe choose Aya
        # But manual analysis earlier determined only index 0 had both cues and should be Aya.
        # We'll check specific cases: s==124 we choose Aya, else if 'أَيُحِبُّ' substring present -> Aya else default to Hadeeth
        if "أَيُحِب" in raw[s:e] or "يَا" in raw[max(0,s-2):e+2]:
            ctype = "Aya"
        else:
            ctype = "Hadeeth"
    else:
        # no cues; decide by content: if substring starts with Arabic diacritized words like 'أَيُحِبُّ' maybe ayah else hadith depending on context
        # we'll use heuristics: if it contains 'اللَّه' or 'اللَّه' or 'أَيُحِب' -> Aya else Hadeeth
        snippet = raw[s:e]
        if "الل" in snippet or "أَيُحِب" in snippet or "يَا" in snippet:
            ctype = "Aya"
        else:
            ctype = "Hadeeth"

    # Now trim edges
    ts, te = trim_span(s, e)
    span_text = raw[ts:te]
    # enforce rule to remove trailing punctuation e.g question mark at end. Already trimmed.
    # Final check raw[ts:te] == span_text always true by definition.
    final_spans.append((ts, te, span_text, ctype))

# Print results
for fs in final_spans:
    print(fs[0], fs[1], fs[2], fs[3])

# Compose final corrected spans list
corrected = []
# list of tuples as from final_spans but adjust types for certain indices
# Rebuild from earlier final_spans with index mapping
# We'll reconstruct final_spans_by_start for easy update
final_by_start = {fs[0]:[fs[0], fs[1], fs[2], fs[3]] for fs in final_spans}

# Update misclassified starts: 948-> Hadeeth, 1449-> Hadeeth
for start in [948, 1449]:
    if start in final_by_start:
        final_by_start[start][3] = "Hadeeth"

# Ensure other ones are correctly classified (Aya vs Hadeeth)
# Some earlier were assigned 'Aya' for 124 and 2407 and 3361 which are correct
# Now create sorted list
final_list = [tuple(v) for k,v in sorted(final_by_start.items())]

# Verify raw[start:end]==text for each and print
for s,e,text,ctype in final_list:
    assert raw[s:e] == text, f"Mismatch at {s}-{e}: raw slice not equal to text"
    print(s,e,ctype, repr(text[:60]))

len(final_list)

# Update trim_chars to include Arabic question mark and Arabic semicolon maybe already included
trim_chars = set(list(" \n\r\t\u200f\u200e"  # whitespace incl directional marks
                     "«»\"'()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟…'!"  # punctuation and quotes including Arabic ?
                     "ـ"  # tatweel
                    ))
# Now re-trim all candidates and generate final spans with classification as decided
final_spans2 = []
# list starts we decided to include (exclude 3907)
include_starts = [124,315,365,398,436,764,810,948,1016,1449,1799,1873,2407,3042,3092,3156,3361,4094]
# mapping for classification as per manual decisions
classification_map = {124:"Aya",315:"Hadeeth",365:"Hadeeth",398:"Hadeeth",436:"Hadeeth",
                      764:"Hadeeth",810:"Hadeeth",948:"Hadeeth",1016:"Hadeeth",1449:"Hadeeth",
                      1799:"Hadeeth",1873:"Hadeeth",2407:"Aya",3042:"Hadeeth",3092:"Hadeeth",
                      3156:"Hadeeth",3361:"Aya",4094:"Hadeeth"}

for s,e,content,orig_type in candidates:
    if s not in include_starts:
        continue
    ts, te = s, e
    # trim
    while ts < te and raw[ts] in trim_chars:
        ts += 1
    while te > ts and raw[te-1] in trim_chars:
        te -= 1
    span_text = raw[ts:te]
    ctype = classification_map.get(ts, "Hadeeth")
    final_spans2.append((ts, te, span_text, ctype))

# Check and print
for fs in final_spans2:
    s,e,text,ctype = fs
    print(s,e,ctype,repr(text))
